steps.title: Fastest path: mark the givens first
steps.content: Count the relevant dimensions or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Apply Pythagorean Theorem to find the other side
steps.content:
The diagonal of a rectangle forms a right triangle with its two adjacent sides. Starting with side $a = 6$ and diagonal $d = \sqrt{85}$:
$$(\sqrt{85})^2 = 6^2 + b^2$$
$$85 = 36 + b^2$$
$$b^2 = 49$$
$$b = 7$$

steps.title: Calculate the perimeter
steps.content:
With both sides now known (6 and 7):
$$P = 2(6 + 7) = 2 \cdot 13 = 26$$
That confirms B is correct.
